name: Honey Nut Rolls ( Bourekakia)
recipeInstructions:
- In a bowl, mix the chopped nuts, sugar, cinnamon, grated orange rind, and melted butter.
- Defrost filo to room temperature at least 2 hours before using.
- On a cutting board, smooth out 3 sheets of filo. Brush warm butter between the sheets.
- Sprinkle about two-thirds of the nut filling over the top, spreading it to the corners.
- Lay another 3 sheets of filo over the filling, brushing butter between them.
- Fold the edges over on each side to keep the nuts from rolling out.
- Starting with the end nearest you, roll away from you as tightly as possible.
- Cut into 1-inch slices. Place each roll in an oiled pan, snugly next to each other, with the nut fillings facing upwards.
- Continue using all the filo and filling.
- When all the slices are in the pan, gently drizzle a teaspoon of warm butter over each slice.
- Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes.
- While the rolls are baking, make the syrup: Combine 2 cups honey, 1 cup water, 2 cups sugar, and a tablespoon lemon juice.
- After removing the rolls from the oven, immediately pour the hot syrup over them using a spoon.
- Allow the rolls to sit with the syrup for 4 hours. Do not refrigerate.
